notes: BG 2.21 names four properties — avināśin, nitya, aja, avyaya — and asks rhetorically how the realised person can be agent of killing. The engineering layer preserves all four properties as a translated quartet (spec indestructible, contract eternal, protocol unborn, abstraction undecaying) and preserves the rhetorical question's structure: 'how could such a builder become agent of slaying predecessors' modules, or be slain when their own deprecates?' The doublet 'kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam' (causes-killed / kills) is preserved as the doublet of agency over predecessors' code and over one's own deprecation — both denied. Shankara's general-denial-of-agency reading and Ramanuja's 'cause the death of any embodied self' reading are both honoured by the analog framing. No Sanskrit term is mistranslated. The translation field uses 'realised craftsperson' as a careful render of puruṣaḥ-the-knower; this is consistent with Shankara's reading of puruṣaḥ as the man-of-realization whose adhikāra is jñāna-niṣṭhā. PASS.
